summary.text: Provides extension methods for adding the Azure SQL resources to the application model.

members.name: AddAzureSqlServer

summary.text: Adds an Azure SQL Database (server) resource to the application model.

builder.text: The builder for the distributed application.

summary.text: Adds an Azure SQL Database to the application model. The Free Offer option will be used when deploying the resource in Azure

builder.text: The builder for the Azure SQL resource.

databaseName.text: The name of the database. If not provided, this defaults to the same value as

name.text: The name of the resource. This name will be used as the connection string name when referenced in a dependency.

members.name: RunAsContainer
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<AzureSqlServerResource> AzureSqlExtensions.RunAsContainer(this IResourceBuilder<AzureSqlServerResource> builder, Action<IResourceBuilder<SqlServerServerResource>>? configureContainer = null)

summary.text: Configures an Azure SQL Database (server) resource to run locally in a container.

remarks.text: The following example creates an Azure SQL Database (server) resource that runs locally in a SQL Server container and referencing that resource in a .NET project.

builder.text: The builder for the Azure SQL resource.

configureContainer.text: Callback that exposes underlying container to allow for customization.

members.name: WithAdminDeploymentScriptStorage
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<AzureSqlServerResource> AzureSqlExtensions.WithAdminDeploymentScriptStorage(this IResourceBuilder<AzureSqlServerResource> builder, IResourceBuilder<AzureStorageResource> storage)

summary.text: Configures the Azure SQL Server to use the specified storage account for deployment script execution.

children.text: When an Azure SQL Server has a private endpoint, deployment scripts require a storage account to upload scripts and write logs. This method allows you to provide an explicit storage account instead of having one auto-created.
enabled, as deployment scripts need to mount file shares. If the storage is not an existing resource, this method will automatically configure

builder.text: The Azure SQL Server resource builder.

storage.text: The storage account to use for deployment scripts.

description.text: Provide a custom storage account for the deployment script:

members.name: WithAdminDeploymentScriptSubnet
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<AzureSqlServerResource> AzureSqlExtensions.WithAdminDeploymentScriptSubnet(this IResourceBuilder<AzureSqlServerResource> builder, IResourceBuilder<AzureSubnetResource> subnet)

summary.text: Configures the Azure SQL Server to use the specified subnet for deployment script execution.

children.text: When an Azure SQL Server has a private endpoint, deployment scripts that add database role assignments run inside Azure Container Instances (ACI). This method allows you to provide an explicit subnet for those containers instead of having one auto-created.
The specified subnet will be automatically delegated to
. Ensure the subnet has outbound network security rules allowing access to Azure Active Directory (port 443) and SQL (port 443) service tags.
This method is not available in polyglot app hosts.

builder.text: The Azure SQL Server resource builder.

subnet.text: The subnet to delegate for Azure Container Instances used by deployment scripts.

description.text: Provide a custom ACI subnet for the deployment script:

summary.text: Configures the Azure SQL Database to be deployed use the default SKU provided by Azure. Please be aware that the Azure default Sku might not take advantage of the free offer.

builder.text: The builder for the Azure SQL resource.

summary.text: A resource that represents an Azure SQL database. This is a child resource of an

members.name: .ctor
members.signature: public AzureSqlDatabaseResource.AzureSqlDatabaseResource(string name, string databaseName, AzureSqlServerResource parent)

summary.text: A resource that represents an Azure SQL database. This is a child resource of an

parent.text: The Azure SQL Database (server) parent resource associated with this database.

remarks.text: This property is not available in polyglot app hosts.

summary.text: Gets the connection string expression for the Azure SQL database.
Gets the database name.
Gets a value indicating whether the current resource represents a container. If so the actual resource is not running in Azure.
Gets the JDBC connection string for the Azure SQL database.

remarks.text: Format:
jdbc:sqlserver://{host}:{port};database={database};encrypt=true;trustServerCertificate=false
. When running in a container, inherits the container JDBC format including username and password.

summary.text: Gets the parent Azure SQL Database (server) resource.
Gets the connection URI expression for the Azure SQL database.

remarks.text: Format:
mssql://{host}:{port}/{database}
